_note: Synthetic vectors authored via the oracle method: the ATIM upstream codecpir.yaml ships no example payloads, so inputs are hand-built per the upstream ACW frame format (vendor/atim/decodeur.js) and expected.data is the normalized output this codec must produce. byte0 0xA0 = new-product measurement frame; channel marker low nibble selects type (0x01 digital input = PIR detection line, 0x04 = 32-bit motion counter). 0x80/0x01 = life frame (battery), 0x80/0x0E = error frame.

uplink.description: measurement: PIR detection on input 0 (raw input bits, bit0 set)
measurement: no detection (raw input bits all clear)
measurement: per-channel motion state byte (>15, movement-present + state-high) on input 0
measurement: detection plus counter (counting mode), detected=false count=42
life frame: battery 3.333 V, capacitor 2.56 V
error frame: critical battery (ERR_BATTERY_LEVEL_DEAD 0x8A) with appended voltage 3.2 V
legacy ATIM product frame (byte0 high bit clear) is rejected
